title: Minimization of Boole Functions Using Karnaugh Diagrams and Combinational Circuits
main_topics: II.4. Minimization of Boole Functions Using Karnaugh Diagrams

definitions.description: A 2-dimensional table for visualizing Boole functions where adjacent cells differ by exactly one variable (Grey code ordering).
A binary encoding where any two consecutive values differ by exactly one bit. Used for labeling Karnaugh diagram rows/columns.
Input combinations that will never occur, allowing freedom to assign 0 or 1 to simplify the Boole expression.
A combinational circuit with 2^n data inputs, n selection inputs, and one output. Routes one of the data inputs to the output based on the selection bits.
A combinational circuit with n inputs and 2^n outputs. At each moment exactly one output is active, corresponding to the binary value on the input.
Adds two 1-bit inputs; outputs a sum bit and a carry-out bit.
Adds three 1-bit inputs (including carry-in); outputs a sum bit and carry-out. Can be cascaded for multi-bit addition.
A multi-bit adder built by cascading full adders; carry propagates sequentially. Simple but slow.
An adder that computes carry bits independently and in parallel, reducing propagation delay.

formulas.name: Karnaugh block size
formulas.description: A block of 2^k positions in a Karnaugh diagram yields a conjunction term of n-k variables.

formulas.name: Carry lookahead
formulas.expression: C_i = G_i + P_i * C_{i-1} = A_i * B_i + (A_i + B_i) * C_{i-1}

algorithms.name: Karnaugh minimization
steps: Fill the Karnaugh diagram with function values
Find all maximal blocks of 1s (each must be of size 2^k)
Cover all 1s with as few and as large blocks as possible
For each block, write the conjunction term using the variables that remain constant across the block

algorithms.name: MUX-based Boole function implementation (folding)
algorithms.description: Use half the variables as selection inputs; assign the remaining variable (or its complement or 0/1) to the data inputs.

examples.name: Majority of 3 – Karnaugh
examples.description: Minimal expression BC + AC + AB derived via Karnaugh.

examples.name: 7-segment display digit decoder
examples.description: Segment 'd' truth table with impossible combinations; two implementations (safe vs. exploiting don't cares).

examples.name: 2-bit comparison circuit
examples.description: 4 inputs (A,B,C,D) for numbers N1=AB and N2=CD; outputs LT, EQ, GT.

examples.name: BCD increment-by-1 circuit
examples.description: Add 1 to a BCD digit (0–9); impossible combinations for 10–15.

exercises: 2-bit comparison: derive minimal expressions for LT, EQ, GT using Karnaugh
2-bit multiplication: derive expressions for P8, P4, P2, P1
BCD increment: derive expressions using impossible combinations
learning_objectives: Apply the Veitch-Karnaugh method to minimize Boole functions
Understand Grey code and diagram adjacencies
Handle impossible input combinations in circuit design
Design multiplexers and implement Boole functions using them
Build adder circuits (half-adder, full adder, ripple-carry)
Compare ripple-carry and carry lookahead adders
Understand the structure of an ALU
